brief_title: Mepolizumab in Children and Adolescents with Severe Eosinophilic Asthma
official_title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Mepolizumab (Anti-IL5 Monoclonal Antibody) in Children and Adolescents Aged 6-17 Years with Severe Eosinophilic Asthma
interventions: Mepolizumab 40mg (6-11 years) or 100mg (12-17 years) subcutaneous injection every 4 weeks
brief_summary: This Phase 3 study evaluates the efficacy and safety of mepolizumab in children and adolescents aged 6-17 with severe eosinophilic asthma who have had at least 2 exacerbations in the past year despite medium-to-high dose inhaled corticosteroids. The primary endpoint is the annualized rate of clinically significant asthma exacerbations.
raw_inclusion_text:
1. Children and adolescents aged 6-17 years at screening

2. Physician-diagnosed asthma for at least 12 months

3. On medium-to-high dose inhaled corticosteroids plus at least one additional controller for >= 3 months

4. Blood eosinophil count >= 150 cells/μL at screening or >= 300 cells/μL within the past 12 months

5. At least 2 asthma exacerbations requiring systemic corticosteroids or ER visit/hospitalization in the past 12 months

6. Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 < 90% predicted at screening

7. Documented reversibility (FEV1 increase >= 12% post-bronchodilator) or positive methacholine challenge within past 12 months

8. Ability to perform reproducible spirometry

9. Parent/guardian capable of providing informed consent

10. Assent from participant where appropriate per local regulations

11. Willingness to maintain current asthma medications during run-in
raw_exclusion_text:
1. Current smoker or former smoker with >= 5 pack-year history (for adolescents >= 14 years)

2. Other chronic lung disease (cystic fibrosis, bronchiectasis, primary ciliary dyskinesia)

3. Asthma exacerbation or respiratory infection within 4 weeks of screening

4. Prior use of any biologic therapy for asthma

5. Known immunodeficiency or chronic immunosuppressive therapy

6. Parasitic infection within 6 months

7. Known hypersensitivity to mepolizumab or excipients

8. Participation in another interventional study within 30 days or 5 half-lives

9. Body weight < 20 kg at screening

inclusion_criteria.text: Children and adolescents aged 6-17 years at screening
Physician-diagnosed asthma for at least 12 months
On medium-to-high dose inhaled corticosteroids plus at least one additional controller for >= 3 months
Blood eosinophil count >= 150 cells/μL at screening or >= 300 cells/μL within the past 12 months
At least 2 asthma exacerbations requiring systemic corticosteroids or ER visit/hospitalization in the past 12 months
Documented reversibility (FEV1 increase >= 12% post-bronchodilator) or positive methacholine challenge within past 12 months
Parent/guardian capable of providing informed consent
Assent from participant where appropriate per local regulations
Willingness to maintain current asthma medications during run-in

exclusion_criteria.text: Current smoker or former smoker with >= 5 pack-year history (for adolescents >= 14 years)
Other chronic lung disease (cystic fibrosis, bronchiectasis, primary ciliary dyskinesia)
Asthma exacerbation or respiratory infection within 4 weeks of screening
Prior use of any biologic therapy for asthma
Known immunodeficiency or chronic immunosuppressive therapy
Known hypersensitivity to mepolizumab or excipients
Participation in another interventional study within 30 days or 5 half-lives
